title: Memory page shows lifecycle proof for captured repo knowledge
summary: Kage viewer memory page now shows a Lifecycle proof section that turns packets into a capture grounding recall handoff story. This was added after comparing session lifecycle visibility requirements; Kage should prove collab
body:
Kage viewer memory page now shows a Lifecycle proof section that turns packets into a capture -> grounding -> recall -> handoff story. This was added after comparing session lifecycle visibility requirements; Kage should prove collaborative repo memory through grounded packet lifecycle cards, not raw transcript replay. The section ranks real memory packets by code links, evidence, recall query usefulness, and handoff value.
Evidence: Implemented in mcp/viewer/memory.html, mcp/viewer/app.js, mcp/viewer/styles.css and synced to docs/viewer assets; asset cache bumped to v=35.
Verified by: node --check mcp/viewer/app.js; node --check docs/viewer/app.js; npm test --prefix mcp
